data.title: Can Robinhood Stock Hit $150 Amid Ark Invest’s Bet & This Major Update?
data.description:
Key Insights: Robinhood stock has recorded a surge of over 5% in the pre-market trading session today, soaring past the $120 mark. Notably, the surge in HOOD stock price came despite a muted sentiment recorded in the broader crypto market, and BTC price traded below $87,000.
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/11/27/can-robinhood-stock-hit-150-amid-ark-invests-bet-this-major-update/

data.title: More than half of Monad airdrop recipients have already "cleared out" their stock, with only one-third choosing to continue holding.
data.description: According to Adam's statistics, among the 76,021 wallets that received the Monad airdrop, 39,796 wallets (52.4%) have sold or transferred all their tokens; 27,133 wallets (35.7%) remain fully invested; 5,728 wallets (7.5%) have sold/transferred more than 50% of their tokens; and another 3,364 wallets (4.4%) have sold/transferred less than 50%. Overall, the data shows that "cash-out" is the dominant group among airdrop recipients, with long-term holders accounting for less than 40%.

data.title: Trader Faces Significant Losses Amid Market Volatility
data.description:
According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, recent monitoring of popular addresses revealed that a trader known as 'Calm Order King' closed all BTC long positions within the past two hours, securing a modest profit of $78,000. The funds were then redirected to increase ZEC long positions.

Further monitoring indicated that at 1:00 (UTC+8), the trader experienced a substantial liquidation due to a brief surge in BTC prices, resulting in a loss of approximately $1.52 million. The account balance was reduced to $300,000, prompting a shift towards opening long positions in BTC, SOL, ZEC, and MON, with a total position size of around $2.9 million. The account currently shows a minor floating loss of $60,000. The primary long positions include $1.68 million in ZEC, $750,000 in SOL, and $520,000 in MON.

Over the past two months, the trader has grown an initial capital of $3 million to over $30 million, gaining attention for accurately predicting market trends. However, recent activity has led to three significant liquidations, with losses totaling $18.46 million over seven days.

data.title: Nexton Solutions Secures $4 Million in Strategic Funding
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Nexton Solutions, an AI-native re-staking and arbitrage execution protocol, has successfully raised $4 million in strategic funding. The investment round was led by South Korean payment company Danal, with participation from Amber Group, Value Systems, Metalabs Ventures, Vista Labs, Outlier Ventures, Kaia Foundation, TON Foundation, STON.fi, and PayProtocol.

Nexton Solutions is developing a unified AI execution layer aimed at automating cross-chain yield generation through its core components.

data.title: Animoca Brands Eyes Public Listing Amid Altcoin Market Expansion
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Animoca Brands founder Yat Siu is positioning his company as a gateway for investors to access a broader range of crypto projects and the altcoin market. The company plans to go public through a reverse merger next year. Siu expressed confidence in altcoins, suggesting they will collectively outperform Bitcoin over time. He likened Bitcoin to gold, noting that while no single company surpasses gold's market capitalization, the combined value of all public companies is significantly higher. Despite the volatility of these companies compared to gold, Siu emphasized the utility of altcoins across various sectors, including Web3 games, gas fees, decentralized finance (DeFi), and decentralized physical infrastructure networks (DePIN).

Siu highlighted Animoca Brands' strategy to offer investment opportunities akin to those available during the early 2000s with major internet companies like Amazon, Google, eBay, and Alibaba. He believes the crypto industry will not have a singular dominant altcoin or project, unlike the early internet era. Animoca Brands aims to capture some of the successful ventures within the crypto space through its extensive investment portfolio. The company has invested in 628 companies, with a significant portion focused on gaming-related projects. Additionally, there is growing investment in infrastructure, artificial intelligence, and DeFi.

Siu noted that Animoca Brands can invest in altcoins at lower levels than external investors, providing shareholders with the advantage of accessing these opportunities. This development follows Cointelegraph's recent report on Animoca Brands' plans for a Nasdaq listing next year via a reverse merger with the AI-focused public fintech company Currenc Group. The move is part of Animoca's broader strategy to expand its presence in the altcoin market and offer investors exposure to diverse crypto projects.

data.title: Nexton Solutions raises $4 million in strategic funding, led by Danal.
data.description: Nexton Solutions, a native AI restaking and arbitrage execution protocol, has raised $4 million in strategic funding, led by Danal, with participation from Amber Group, Value Systems, Metalabs Ventures, Vista Labs, Outlier Ventures, Kaia Foundation, TON Foundation, STON.fi, and PayProtocol. Nexton is building a unified AI execution layer, including the Nexton-ai cross-DEX/CEX arbitrage routing engine and the Nexton-re automatic restaking module, and providing full-chain yield services through the native Telegram environment. The platform currently has a TVL exceeding $3 million, approximately 60,000 monthly active users, and annualized returns for its AI strategies ranging from 70% to 90%. (Chainwire)

data.title: Ripple USD Recognized as Fiat-Anchored Token in Abu Dhabi
data.description: According to PANews, Ripple has announced that its USD-backed stablecoin, Ripple USD (RLUSD), has been recognized by the Abu Dhabi Financial Services Regulatory Authority (FSRA) as a 'recognized fiat-anchored token.' This designation allows RLUSD to be used within the Abu Dhabi Global Market (ADGM), the international financial center in the capital of the United Arab Emirates (UAE).

data.title: Australia Proposes Digital Asset Framework to Enhance Customer Protection
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, Australia's Treasurer Jim Chalmers and Minister for Financial Services Daniel Mulino presented the 2025 Corporate Amendment (Digital Assets Framework) Bill to Parliament on Wednesday. The legislation aims to address regulatory gaps and enhance the protection of billions in customer assets.

The bill seeks to bring cryptocurrency exchanges and custodial service providers under the regulatory scope of financial services laws, designating the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) as the primary regulatory body. The new framework introduces two new categories of financial products: digital asset platforms and tokenized custodial platforms, requiring operators to hold an 'Australian Financial Services License.' These platforms must operate 'efficiently, honestly, and fairly,' adhering to ASIC standards concerning asset security, transaction execution, and client instruction handling.

The bill includes exemption clauses for low-risk platforms holding assets below $5,000 per client and with annual transaction volumes under $10,000,000, allowing them to bypass full licensing requirements. The government anticipates this move will unlock $24 billion in productivity growth annually, while imposing multi-million dollar fines on companies failing to protect customer assets.

data.title: Australia passes new consumer protection bill, bringing crypto platforms under financial services regulation.
data.description: Australian Treasurer Jim Chalmers and Financial Services Minister Daniel Mulino introduced the Companies Amendment (Digital Asset Framework) Bill 2025 to Parliament on Wednesday, aiming to address regulatory gaps and strengthen the protection of billions of dollars in customer assets. The bill brings crypto exchanges and custody service providers under the purview of financial services law and designates the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) as the primary regulator. The new framework introduces two new categories of financial products: digital asset platforms and tokenized custody platforms, requiring operators to hold an “Australian Financial Services License.” These platforms must operate “efficiently, honestly, and impartially” and comply with ASIC’s custody and settlement standards regarding asset security, trade execution, and processing of customer instructions. The bill includes exemptions: low-risk platforms with less than $5,000 in assets held per customer and an annual trading volume of less than $10 million are exempt from the full licensing requirements. The government says this move is expected to unlock $24 billion in productivity growth annually, while imposing millions of dollars in fines on companies that fail to protect customer assets. (Decrypt)

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $21.1228 million yesterday, with only Fidelity FBTC experiencing a net outflow.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $21.1228 million yesterday (November 26th, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF IBIT saw the largest single-day net inflow of $42.8249 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $62.68 billion. Following closely were Ark Invest and 21Shares' ARKB ETF, with a net inflow of $5.9685 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $1.74 billion. The Fidelity ETF FBTC saw the largest single-day net outflow of $33.2968 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $11.951 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $117.664 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) is 6.56%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $57.634 billion.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $60.8176 million yesterday, marking the fourth consecutive day of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $60.8176 million yesterday (November 26th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was BlackRock ETF ETHA, with a net inflow of $50.2174 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $13.079 billion. This was followed by Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETF ETH, with a single-day net inflow of $6.2745 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $1.449 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs was $18.88 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of total Ethereum market capitalization) of 5.17%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $12.868 billion.
